topic: fell running gear — waterproof jacket recommendations

turns.content: right so I need a decent waterproof for running in the hills. not a walking jacket. an actual running one. whats good these days.

For fell running specifically, you want something that's lightweight, packable, and breathable but still properly waterproof -- not just shower-resistant. A few solid options:

- **Inov-8 Stormshell** -- very popular in the fell running world, around 200g, fully taped seams, packs down small.
- **OMM Halo+** -- ultralight at about 115g, eVent fabric so it breathes really well. Minimal but does the job.
- **Montane Minimus Lite** -- Gore-Tex Paclite, good balance of protection and weight. Around 180g.
- **Rab Phantom Pull-On** -- if you don't mind a smock-style. Very light, very packable.

What kind of conditions are you running in? And are you looking at something for race-mandatory kit or daily training?

daily training mostly. I run in the highlands so conditions are basically hostile sept through march. horizontal rain. 40mph wind on the ridges. needs to actually work not just look good on a mannequin in cotswold outdoor.

Ha, fair point -- Highland weather is a different beast from what most jackets are tested for. In that case I'd lean away from the ultralight options like the OMM Halo+. They're brilliant for racing but they won't hold up to sustained horizontal rain and gale-force wind on a regular basis.

For your conditions I'd look at:

- **Inov-8 Stormshell** -- probably the best all-rounder for consistent bad weather. Pertex Shield fabric, 3-layer construction, properly windproof. It's about 200g so not heavy.
- **Montane Minimus Lite** -- Gore-Tex Paclite holds up well to prolonged rain. Hood fits well over a cap, which helps in driving rain.
- **Gore R7 Shakedry** -- this is the one if budget isn't a concern. Water literally beads off it. Doesn't wet out. No DWR to reapply. Fragile though, so you'd want to be careful with pack straps.

What's your budget roughly?
prob 150-200 quid. dont mind paying for something thatll last. the shakedry thing sounds interesting but if its fragile thats a problem. im not exactly gentle with my kit tbh.

Yeah, Shakedry is amazing fabric but it doesn't love abrasion -- rubbing against a pack or catching it on a fence will shorten its life. If you're rough on gear, skip it.

At the 150-200 range, the Inov-8 Stormshell (around 160-170) is probably your best bet. It's built for people who are actually out in it, not just carrying it in a bag for checkpoints. The Pertex Shield fabric is durable enough for regular use and it handles sustained wind and rain well.

The Montane Minimus Lite sits around 150 and is a solid second choice. Slightly more breathable than the Inov-8 but the hood isn't quite as good in high wind.

One tip -- whatever you get, make sure the hem and cuffs seal well. With horizontal rain, water ingress through gaps is usually the problem, not the fabric itself failing.

aye thats a good point about the hem. my old running jacket was basically a wind funnel from the waist down. useless. whats the inov8 hood like. can you cinch it properly or is it one of those wee floppy things.

The Stormshell hood is decent -- it has a single rear adjuster so you can cinch it down around your face, and it's peaked so it doesn't collapse in wind. It's helmet-compatible too, which means it has enough volume to fit over a cap or buff without feeling like it's strangling you.

It's not the best hood in fell running -- the OMM Kamleika has probably the best hood out there with a wired peak -- but the Stormshell hood is solid and stays put when you tighten it. Much better than the floppy sock-type hoods you get on some ultralight jackets.

One thing to check: some people find the rear adjuster a bit fiddly with cold wet hands. If you can try it in a shop, worth testing that. But overall it's one of the better running jacket hoods at that price point.
fiddly with cold wet hands. so every single time id use it then. brilliant. is the kamleika worth a look or is that more of a walking jacket.

No, the Kamleika is proper running kit -- it's actually one of the most popular jackets in UK fell running. OMM designed it specifically for mountain running. It's a softshell-waterproof hybrid -- the fabric is stretchy and very breathable, more so than any hardshell.

The trade-off: it's not as waterproof as a 3-layer hardshell in truly torrential, sustained rain. It'll handle most conditions brilliantly, but in a proper Highland deluge it will eventually wet through. It's more of a "comfortable in 90% of conditions" jacket than a "bomb-proof in 100%" jacket.

Price is around 160-180 so right in your range. If you run in heavy rain regularly and want absolute waterproofness, stick with the Stormshell. If you value comfort and breathability and can tolerate getting a bit damp in the worst storms, the Kamleika is hard to beat.

Honestly, a lot of serious fell runners own both and pick based on the forecast.

own both. aye thats the dream. might start with the stormshell given where I am and what the weather does here. can always add the kamleika later for the nicer days. when its only raining sideways and not also trying to knock you off the hill.

Sound logic. The Stormshell as your workhorse for the worst of it, then the Kamleika for when conditions are merely awful rather than apocalyptic. That's a good setup.

One last thing -- if you're ordering online, Inov-8's sizing can run a touch small, especially in the chest. Might be worth going up half a size if you're between sizes, or if you plan to layer underneath. Their return process is straightforward though if it doesn't fit.

Hope it serves you well out there.
cheers thats actually helpful. ill order the stormshell and see how it goes. if it keeps me dry on ben wyvis in october ill consider it a success.
Ben Wyvis in October is about as good a stress test as any jacket could ask for. If it survives that, it'll handle anything. Good luck with it -- and with the weather.
